behavior_summary: When the player examines/looks at this object, the script emits a short descriptive bark: "shelf ".
Immediately after emitting the bark the process yields/terminates via a suspend — the U8P_NSSHFSID::look handler does not perform further logic, branching, or external calls.
notable_control_flow: Single linear flow: U8P_NSSHFSID::look posts a Bark (text "shelf ") then reaches a suspend, ending or yielding the U8P_NSSHFSID::look process.
No conditional branches, menu logic, or calls to external systems are present in the extracted script.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "shelf " intentional or an artifact of extraction?
Does the suspend represent an engine-standard end-of-process (common for U8P_NSSHFSID::look handlers) or is it used here to explicitly yield for resume later?
Is this U8P_NSSHFSID::look handler attached to an item instance or a static object/NPC in the world (the script excerpt does not show ownership/context)?
